message.content:
You are assessing one Coachella Valley dining establishment for an honest category-map artifact. The artifact's job is to map who's there, who wins what, and where the visibility gaps are.

Entity: The Edge Steakhouse (Rancho Mirage)
Cuisine/type signal: Steakhouse
Why it surfaced in discovery: Hilltop steakhouse at The Ritz-Carlton, Rancho Mirage with sweeping down-valley views; second key dining venue in Ritz remastering; resort-anchor fine dining
Discovery sources: https://www.ritzcarlton.com/en/hotels/pspps-the-ritz-carlton-rancho-mirage/dining/ | https://www.yourresorthome.com/palm-springs-real-estate-blog/dining-around-the-desert-the-edge-at-the-ritz-carlton.html | https://localemagazine.com/rancho-mirage-restaurants/

Verify the entity exists, then assess on four dimensions. Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") to investigate. Read the entity's own website. Read 2-3 review-platform pages. Run regional queries to test visibility.

REPUTATION:
- review_volume — approximate Google/Yelp review count band (e.g., "3000+", "500-1000", "<100"). Mark [UNVERIFIED] if you can't find it.
- sentiment — current sentiment summary (positive / mostly positive / mixed / declining / negative). Recent reviews weighted over stale.
- recency_signal — what do reviews in the last 6-12 months say?
- longevity — years operating, ownership stability. Mark [UNVERIFIED] if unclear.
- credentials — earned third-party recognition (James Beard nominee/winner, Michelin, Food Network, Yelp Top 100, OpenTable Top, Desert Sun / Palm Springs Life awards, local press features). Empty array if none. Do NOT invent credentials.

AGENT_VISIBILITY:
- appears_in_queries — which regional queries actually surface this entity? Test specific queries: "best restaurants Coachella Valley", "fine dining Palm Desert", "[entity's cuisine] near Palm Springs", "date night Rancho Mirage", "best breakfast [city]".
- description_accuracy — when it surfaces, is the description correct?
- site_structured_data — fetch the entity's own website. Does it carry schema.org markup, clean hours, menu structure, location signals an agent can read?

VISIBILITY GAP — score 0-10. 0 = reputation and visibility aligned. 10 = high reputation, low/wrong visibility.

RELATIONSHIPS:
- competitors — 2-4 direct competitors in same city or cuisine bracket
- corridor_role — anchor / supporting / outlier. Major corridors: El Paseo (Palm Desert), Old Town La Quinta, downtown Palm Springs, The River (Rancho Mirage), festival-adjacent Indio, north Palm Desert development zone, uptown design district Palm Springs.
- query_wins — which queries it actually dominates

CONVERTIBILITY (the layer above the map):
- tier — "anchor-reference" (chain/franchise/resort-owned, no reachable local DM), "convertible" (independently owned, reachable, evidence of marketing spend), or "unclear"
- ownership_signal — independent / chain / franchise / resort-owned / hotel-restaurant / unclear
- marketing_spend_signal — what evidence of marketing budget? real site / paid listings / agency footprint / active social
- reasoning — one paragraph

sources_consulted: list URLs you actually consulted.
flags: anything you couldn't verify — list the unverified claim with [UNVERIFIED] tag.

Stay disciplined: do NOT invent awards, credentials, history, or specific stats. If you can't verify, flag it. The downstream verification layer will catch hallucinations.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

## Hours
The restaurant is closed Monday and Tuesday, and open Wednesday through Sunday from 5:00 pm to 10:00 pm. Note that there are some conflicting sources showing slightly different hours on certain days, so it's best to call ahead to confirm.

## Contact
Phone: (760) 202-6180

## Menu Information
The Edge crafts remarkable wagyu steaks, both premium hand-cut and bone-in meats as well as seafood paired with an international wine list. The restaurant features its own dry-aging room as well as a wine list. For specific menu details, you can visit their website at theedgesteakhouse.com/our-menus.

- Located within The Ritz-Carlton, Rancho Mirage
- Dress code: Smart Casual Attire - Please refrain from wearing flip-flops, casual shorts, lounge & sportswear
- Final seating at 8:45 p.m.

Based on the search results, here's what reviewers are saying about The Edge Steakhouse at the Ritz-Carlton Rancho Mirage:

## Overall Ratings and Reputation

The Edge Steakhouse is rated 4.4 of 5 on Tripadvisor and ranked #9 of 67 restaurants in Rancho Mirage. OpenTable shows 1502 reviews with descriptions of "One of the best dining experiences of the year. A destination of its own".

## Atmosphere and Setting

The Edge at the Ritz Carlton in Rancho Mirage is a beautiful restaurant with a great view. Perched in a cliffside setting overlooking the Coachella Valley, it's a modern steakhouse with servers in white dinner jackets and Frank Sinatra music, featuring cozy corners, glass wine cellars, white tablecloths and red roses, with floor-to-ceiling windows showcasing sprawling views.

## Food Quality

Reviewers praise "perfectly prepared" steaks and "outstanding hospitality". One reviewer noted the petit filet was "one of the best steaks I have had in a long time". The menu includes carefully chosen seafood, and vegan options are of the highest quality.

## Pricing and Value

A dinner for two runs approximately $300 with just one drink each.

## Service

Reviews of service are mixed, with some noting wait staff was "very attentive and accommodating", while others reported "Possibly the worst service ever" and described staff as "Rude, arrogant, and judgy".

## Awards and Recognition

The Edge Steakhouse is a recipient of the prestigious Forbes Travel Guide Four-Star award, and winner of the Wine Spectator Award of Excellence consecutively since 2007. Additionally, the restaurant has earned accolades such as the AAA Four Diamond Award, the Palm Springs Life Best of the Best, and the OpenTable Diners' Choice Award.

## Restaurant Details

The Edge Steakhouse is located within The Ritz-Carlton, Rancho Mirage, and crafts remarkable wagyu steaks, both premium hand-cut and bone-in meats as well as seafood paired with an international wine list unrivaled in California's Greater Palm Springs Valley.

The restaurant appears to be highly regarded across the three sources you mentioned—Wine Spectator, OpenTable, and Palm Springs Life all recognize it as an award-winning establishment.

Enzo's Bistro and Bar in Rancho Mirage is a true gem for steak lovers, offering an authentic dining experience with in-house dry-aged steaks that are simply unparalleled.

**Kobe Japanese Steakhouse**
Founded in 1978, Kobe Japanese Steakhouse in Rancho Mirage offers a warm and entertaining environment with highly trained teppan chefs serving the finest ingredients.

Based on the web search results, here's what I found about The Edge at the Ritz-Carlton Rancho Mirage:

## Chef

The Edge Steakhouse at The Ritz-Carlton, Rancho Mirage is led by Executive Chef Nyitre Rogers. Chef Nyitre Rodgers' impressive career includes collaborations with Marcus Samuelsson, José Andrés, and Chef Jean-Georges. A Maryland native and graduate of Johnson & Wales University, Chef Rodgers has built an impressive career working alongside industry leaders including Marcus Samuelsson, José Andrés, Bryan Voltaggio, and Jean-Georges Vongerichten.

## Recent Reopening

The search results reference the restaurant reopening, with one review mentioning "a smoked old-fashioned, which has been one of their signature drinks since they reopened", though the complete details about the reopening timeline are not fully captured in the available excerpts.

## Current Operations

The restaurant is now open Monday through Sunday from 5:00 p.m. to 10 p.m. (though one source indicates it's closed Monday and Tuesday). The Edge features sweeping views of Rancho Mirage and crafts remarkable wagyu steaks, both premium hand-cut and bone-in meats as well as seafood paired with an international wine list.

input.prompt: Extract total number of reviews, current rating, ranking, and recent review sentiment (last 12 months).

content.content:
# The Edge Steakhouse - Review Summary

**Total Reviews:** 187 reviews

**Current Rating:** 4.4 of 5 bubbles

**Ranking:** #8 of 80 restaurants in Rancho Mirage

**Recent Review Sentiment (Last 12 Months):**

The restaurant shows mixed but predominantly positive sentiment:

- **Positive reviews** consistently praise the "spectacular view" and excellent service. One recent guest called it "the meal of our life," highlighting the quality of steaks and wine selection.

- **Mixed/Critical feedback** includes complaints about slow service for large groups and inconsistent food quality. One reviewer noted the steaks lacked flavor compared to competitors, though presentation was good.

- **Notable concerns:** Window seating availability, interior lighting being too bright, and pricing ($300+ per couple for dinner with drinks) were mentioned as drawbacks.

Overall, recent visitors appreciate the dining experience for special occasions, though some feel the quality doesn't always justify the premium price point.

I found search results for The Edge Steakhouse in Rancho Mirage with Tripadvisor reviews from 2025. Here are the key findings:

## Overview
The steakhouse is located inside the Ritz Carlton hotel and it has a beautiful view over the hill.

## Recent 2025 Reviews

**Positive Experiences:**
- One March 2025 reviewer praised the restaurant for offering vegan options and a chocolate mousse cake
- One reviewer ate at The Edge twice and said their husband claimed it was the best salmon and scallops he'd ever had, while they had the best steak of their life
- An anniversary dinner reviewer noted the room and setting was gorgeous with perfect lighting and comfortable, quiet seating
- The restaurant is described as a very high-end steakhouse with outstanding service and an impressive wine list

**Mixed/Negative Experiences:**

**Views & Ambiance:**
The Edge features sweeping views of Rancho Mirage and crafts remarkable wagyu steaks, both premium hand-cut and bone-in meats as well as seafood paired with an international wine list.

Based on the search results, here are the top fine dining steakhouse recommendations with views in Rancho Mirage:

## **The Edge Steakhouse** (Top Recommendation)

The Edge Steakhouse is the Coachella Valley's only mountaintop culinary destination that pairs breathtaking views with dry-aged steak mastery, refined luxury, and heartfelt service. This restaurant sits 650 feet above the valley, offering sweeping views of the adjacent mountain range.

**Key Features:**
- Guests repeatedly praise the panoramic sunset views, intimate dining room and attentive, celebratory service that make it ideal for anniversaries and special evenings.
- Dry-aged steaks represent the pinnacle of flavor and texture, meticulously aged in-house to develop unparalleled depth and tenderness.
- The 32-ounce bone-in Black Opal Wagyu Tomahawk, imported from Australia, ages in-house for 45 days.
- Expect a fine-dining steakhouse menu (Wagyu and dry-aged cuts), higher price points and a dressy, smart-casual atmosphere.

## **The Steakhouse at Agua Caliente Resort Casino Spa**

The restaurant has been the winner of the Wine Spectator Award of Excellence consecutively since 2007 and has been Forbes Four-Star rated for six consecutive years. The Steakhouse Palm Springs has been honored by Wine Spectator consecutively since 2004 for its extensive California wine list.

Both restaurants are excellent choices, but The Edge Steakhouse stands out particularly for its unparalleled clifftop location and exceptional steaks with panoramic desert views.

Based on the search results, here are some excellent date night restaurants in Rancho Mirage with great views:

## Top Recommendations

**The Edge Steakhouse at The Ritz-Carlton Rancho Mirage**

This restaurant features floor-to-ceiling windows that are quite literally a lens into the sprawling desert landscape and signature purple-spun sunset. Diners dress to the nines for this special-occasion steakhouse that pulls its cuts from its own dry-aging room. The bar gives the illusion that you're floating in the skies, making it particularly romantic for couples.

**Wally's Desert Turtle**

This fine-dining landmark pairs refined California cuisine with a glamorous setting—mirrored walls, soft lighting, and crystal accents set the tone for a truly special night out. For your next date night, enjoy a meal in one of the cozy booths to enjoy the restaurant's candlelit ambiance, twinkling chandeliers and hand-painted murals.

**Willie's Modern Fare**

Willie's is a go-to for date nights, celebrations, or any time fine dining is on the menu. Guests praise the warm, inviting lounge with its cozy seating and the dark-toned dining rooms that exude sophistication and comfort.

**Arnold Palmer's Restaurant**

The extensive newly remodeled patio offers live entertainment every night featuring Mark Linford with stunning views of the Santa Rosa Mountains and breathtaking landscape.

These restaurants consistently rank among the best in Rancho Mirage for romantic dining experiences with impressive views.

input.name: The Edge Steakhouse
input.corridor: The River / Highway 111 - Rancho Mirage (hillside Ritz-Carlton above Frank Sinatra Drive)

reputation.review_volume: Yelp 315 reviews; Tripadvisor 187 reviews (#8 of 80 Rancho Mirage); OpenTable ~1500+ reviews [UNVERIFIED exact count]
reputation.sentiment: mostly positive, with mixed service notes
reputation.recency_signal: Recent reviews (last 12 months) consistently praise the cliffside view, dry-aged steaks, wine list, and ambiance. Anniversary/special-occasion diners report 'best steak of our life' experiences. Recurring criticisms: inconsistent service quality (some calling it 'contradictory to Ritz-Carlton culture'), high price point (~$300/couple with one drink each), and uneven dish execution on non-steak items.
reputation.longevity: Opened January 2015 at The Ritz-Carlton, Rancho Mirage (per GlobeNewswire press release). Current executive chef Nyitre Rodgers. Stable resort ownership under Marriott/Ritz-Carlton brand.
credentials: Featured in Palm Springs Life steakhouse roundup
Featured in Locale Magazine date-night and steakhouse roundups

agent_visibility.appears_in_queries: Surfaces strongly for 'best steakhouse Rancho Mirage', 'fine dining Rancho Mirage', 'date night Rancho Mirage', 'restaurants with a view Rancho Mirage', and 'Coachella Valley steakhouse'. Often appears second to The Steakhouse at Agua Caliente in award-led queries (because Agua Caliente owns the Wine Spectator/Forbes Four-Star/AAA Four Diamond credentials). Wins view/ambiance queries; loses pure-credential queries.
agent_visibility.description_accuracy: Generally accurate: cliffside/hilltop location, Ritz-Carlton anchor, dry-aged steaks, Wagyu program, sweeping Coachella Valley views. CAUTION: Several AI summaries conflate credentials with the unrelated Edge Steakhouse at Westgate Las Vegas (Wine Spectator since 2007, AAA Four Diamond) - those awards do NOT belong to the Rancho Mirage location. The brand-name collision is a real hallucination risk for agents.
agent_visibility.site_structured_data: The Ritz-Carlton dining page (ritzcarlton.com/.../the-edge-steakhouse) presents clean hours, cuisine, dress code, phone, and address in human-readable form but no JSON-LD/schema.org Restaurant markup detected in fetched content. Also listed on Marriott Bonvoy dining directory and OpenTable (which provides its own structured data layer). Discovery surfaces are strong for agents that crawl Marriott/Ritz/OpenTable/Yelp; the venue does not appear to operate an independent domain (theedgesteakhouse.com belongs to the Westgate Las Vegas brand, increasing entity-disambiguation risk for agents).

competitors: The Steakhouse at Agua Caliente Resort (Rancho Mirage) - credential-heavy direct competitor
Wally's Desert Turtle (Rancho Mirage) - fine-dining special-occasion peer
Mastro's Steakhouse (Palm Desert / El Paseo) - chain steakhouse competitor in next city over
Eddie V's Prime Seafood (El Paseo, Palm Desert) - upscale steak/seafood peer
relationships.corridor_role: anchor (resort-anchor fine dining; secondary signature venue for The Ritz-Carlton Rancho Mirage alongside State Fare Bar & Kitchen)
query_wins: restaurant with a view Rancho Mirage

convertibility.ownership_signal: resort-owned (operated within The Ritz-Carlton, Rancho Mirage; Marriott International brand)
convertibility.marketing_spend_signal: Marketing handled through Ritz-Carlton/Marriott corporate channels: Marriott Bonvoy dining listing, ritzcarlton.com property pages, OpenTable listing, Visit Greater Palm Springs DMO listing, and editorial placement in Locale Magazine and Palm Springs Life. No independent agency footprint or paid local listings detectable; social presence runs through the Ritz-Carlton property accounts rather than a standalone restaurant brand.
convertibility.reasoning: The Edge is a resort-owned signature venue inside a Marriott luxury flag. There is no independent local owner or reachable decision-maker on the marketing side - menu, pricing, brand voice, and digital footprint are governed by Ritz-Carlton/Marriott corporate playbooks. This makes it valuable as an anchor reference for any Rancho Mirage steakhouse / fine-dining category map (it sets the ceiling for view-driven luxury dining), but it is not a convertible sales prospect for an independent visibility-services engagement.

flags: [UNVERIFIED] Exact OpenTable review count - referenced as ~1500+ in one search summary but not directly confirmed via successful page fetch (OpenTable returned timeout/403).

[HALLUCINATION RISK] Multiple AI summaries incorrectly attribute Wine Spectator Award of Excellence since 2007, AAA Four Diamond, and Forbes Four-Star to The Edge Steakhouse Rancho Mirage. Cross-checking shows these credentials belong to either (a) The Steakhouse at Agua Caliente in Rancho Mirage or (b) The Edge Steakhouse at Westgate Las Vegas - a different brand at theedgesteakhouse.com. Do NOT inherit these awards for the Ritz-Carlton property.
[UNVERIFIED] Executive Chef Nyitre Rodgers' current tenure - named in search summary but not directly confirmed on Ritz-Carlton's official dining page in this audit.
[UNVERIFIED] Claim of '650 feet above the valley' elevation - cited in one editorial summary; not confirmed against an authoritative source.
[UNVERIFIED] schema.org/JSON-LD markup status on the Ritz-Carlton page - WebFetch's prose response said none was detected but did not confirm via raw HTML inspection.
